hardware.resource_reference: AMD ZCU104 UG1267 v1.1 and XCZU7EV resource tables

optimization.optimization_brief: Use QuaRot-style rotations so the LLaMA decode path can target W4A4KV4: 4-bit GPTQ weights, 4-bit rotated activations, and 4-bit KV-cache storage. The example assumes GPTQ weight tensors already exist and uses a local sparse metadata fixture only to exercise the parent planning flow.

optimization_candidates.name: quarot_w4a4kv4
optimization_candidates.notes: rotate residual/attention/MLP streams, quantize activations to 4-bit, and store KV-cache in 4-bit form

optimization_candidates.name: w4a8kv4_fallback
optimization_candidates.notes: keep weights and KV-cache at 4-bit but allow 8-bit activations if W4A4 timing or accuracy is unsafe

optimization_candidates.name: mixed_precision_softmax_control
optimization_candidates.notes: keep softmax/control accumulations wider while preserving W4A4KV4 storage boundaries

rotation.implementation_requirement: rotation kernels must be separate non_gemm module packets unless fused by an explicit parent boundary review

design.architecture_brief: Use a weight-stationary systolic array for projection GEMM/GEMV tiles. GPTQ-packed W4 weights are streamed from DDR into on-chip tile buffers, unpacked/dequantized near the array edge, and reused in-place while rotated A4 activations stream through the array. Size the array from the active DSP budget rather than hardcoding a final dimension.

design_candidates.name: ws_systolic_32x32
design_candidates.focus: first candidate under a 1536-DSP active compute budget
design_candidates.risk: routing pressure and activation/KV4 quantization overhead may reduce timing margin

design_candidates.name: ws_systolic_16x64
design_candidates.focus: preserve projection throughput while easing vertical routing
design_candidates.risk: less square data reuse and more edge buffering

design_candidates.name: ws_systolic_24x48
design_candidates.focus: middle-ground DSP use with easier BRAM banking
design_candidates.risk: tile scheduler is less regular than 32x32

systolic_array.sizing_rule: choose rows*cols*dsp_per_cell <= active_dsp_budget after reserving non-GEMM/control DSP

numeric_policy.child_sequence: token_loop_axi_decoder_block_fixture called twice with start_i held while busy and release-waited

steps.timing_margin_note: Prior AXI decoder-block token-loop routed timing had WNS 1.348 ns, WHS 0.009 ns, WPWS 2.225 ns, 132 bonded IOB, and status was 64 bits. This model FSM fixture keeps status at 64 bits and avoids exposing child vectors, AXI data, AXI debug buses, or KV arrays.
steps.verilator_status_policy: Verilator lint result recorded when verification.enable_verilator is true
steps.vivado_timing_status_policy: post-route with non-NA setup/hold/pulse-width when synthesis is enabled
steps.vivado_resource_status_policy: utilization.rpt emitted when Vivado synthesis completes
steps.timing_caveat: Routed timing is fixture timing without board-level I/O delay, DDR/AXI shell, or PS/PL integration constraints; it is not board-level ZCU104 signoff.
omitted_operations: layer-dependent KV-cache accumulation across full sequence length
DDR/AXI packed-weight streaming and full DDR controller integration
true SiLU or exponential activation math in RTL

target.optimization_brief: Use QuaRot-style rotations so the LLaMA decode path can target W4A4KV4: 4-bit GPTQ weights, 4-bit rotated activations, and 4-bit KV-cache storage. The example assumes GPTQ weight tensors already exist and uses a local sparse metadata fixture only to exercise the parent planning flow.

input_clarification.parent_policy: If requires_user_response is true, stop before HDL sub-agent dispatch and ask these questions. If false, preserve the free-form fields in manifests and continue planning.

target_blocker_remediation.canonical_full_preflight_command: python3 -m nl2hdl agent --model meta-llama/Llama-3.2-1B --spec examples/zcu104_llama32_1b_gptq.yaml --mode inspect --out examples/quarot_w4a4kv4_zcu104_llama32_1b/run/framework_e2e_attempt/kernel_cache/model_fsm_axi_decoder_block_fixture --skip-synth --mlir-graph '<exported_or_provided_model_graph.mlir>' --gptq-checkpoint '<local_gptq_int4_checkpoint_dir_or_hf_repo>'

gptq_checkpoint_metadata.quantization_artifact_next_action: run target layout and payload probes for all required LLaMA projections
gptq_checkpoint_metadata.source_preflight_next_action: run GPTQ metadata and payload probes against the resolved local checkpoint directory

gptq_checkpoint_source_preflight.next_action: run GPTQ metadata and payload probes against the resolved local checkpoint directory

blocked_target_tasks.reason: current inspect MLIR does not prove every target LLaMA GEMM/non-GEMM operation from a provided or exported model graph
current gates are bounded fixtures and target planning; full multi-layer/token/DDR/AXI execution is not implemented
current Vivado evidence is fixture post-route timing without board I/O, DDR/AXI, or PS/PL constraints

first_rtl_milestones: MLIR GEMM/non-GEMM partition report for the selected LLaMA checkpoint
INT4xINT8 or INT4xINT16 projection GEMV kernel
single decoder block skeleton with AXI-stream-like tile handshakes
single-token decode loop for one block, then all blocks
acceptance_gates: MLIR/semantic graph report names every LLaMA submodule and tensor shape
GEMM ops are mapped to tiled INT4 projection kernels and non-GEMM ops are mapped to dedicated RMSNorm/RoPE/softmax/control kernels
GPTQ metadata parser round-trips packed INT4 weights for at least one projection
kernel-level RTL matches PyTorch/NumPy references for deterministic vectors
Vivado synthesis reports timing and resource usage on the ZCU104 part
agent report records attempted design knobs and final effective design
